briefdescription.para: Handler for ap_done.
Handler for ap_ready.
These constants specify different types of handler and used to differentiate interrupt requests from peripheral.
SetPowerOnDefaultState - Initializes the frame buffer reader instance to its default power-on state.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance with default video stream parameters, including resolution, color format, frame rate, color depth, and pixels per clock. It sets the hardware registers for frame width, height, stride, and memory video format. If interlaced mode is enabled, it also sets the field ID register. The function disables the DONE and READY interrupts to set up polling mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance to be initialized.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: This function maps the memory video formats to the live/stream video formats.

parameterdescription.para: is the video format read from memory

simplesect.para: Live/Stream Video Format associated with that memory format

briefdescription.para: Initializes the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets up the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance structure, zeroes its memory, and initializes the underlying XV_frmbufrd hardware or driver instance. Depending on the build configuration, it uses either a DeviceId or a BaseAddress to perform the initialization.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance to be initialized.
Device ID of the XV_frmbufrd hardware (used if SDT is not defined).

listitem.para: The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL.
After successful initialization, the instance is set to its power-on default state.

briefdescription.para: Enables specific interrupts for the Frame Buffer Read core and globally enables interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function enables the interrupts specified by the IrqMask for the Frame Buffer Read instance pointed to by InstancePtr. It also globally enables interrupts for the core and disables the autostart feature to ensure manual control of frame buffer operations.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
is the interrupt mask the driver interrupt handler passes to the callback function.

simplesect.para: The InstancePtr must not be NULL.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_InterruptDisable - Disables specific interrupts and enables auto-restart.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the interrupts specified by the IrqMask for the given Frame Buffer Read (FrmbufRd) instance. It also globally disables interrupts for the instance and sets the auto-restart bit to ensure the hardware continues operation without further software intervention.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
is the interrupt mask the driver interrupt handler passes to the callback function.

simplesect.para: The InstancePtr must not be NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the Frame Buffer Read core.

detaileddescription.para: This function initiates the frame buffer read operation by calling the lower-level start function for the frame buffer read hardware. It asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL before proceeding.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_Stop - Stops the Frame Buffer Read core.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the auto-restart feature, initiates a flush operation, and waits for the flush to complete or until a timeout occurs. It returns XST_SUCCESS if the flush completes successfully, or XST_FAILURE if the flush does not complete within the allowed number of attempts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the flush operation completes successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the flush operation times out.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_WaitForIdle - Waits until the Frame Buffer Read core is idle or a timeout occurs.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr :
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
This function polls the Frame Buffer Read core's status until it becomes idle or until a predefined timeout (XVFRMBUFRD_IDLE_TIMEOUT) is reached. It uses the XV_frmbufrd_IsIdle function to check the idle status. If the core becomes idle within the timeout period, the function returns XST_SUCCESS. Otherwise, it returns XST_FAILURE.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the core is in idle state XST_FAILURE if the core is not in idle state

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_FrmbufRd_l2 *InstancePtr, u32 StrideInBytes, XVidC_ColorFormat MemFmt, const XVidC_VideoStream *StrmOut)
memberdef.name: XVFrmbufRd_SetMemFormat

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_SetMemFormat - Configure the memory format and stream parameters for the frame buffer reader.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr :
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
StrideInBytes :
Stride of the frame buffer in bytes. Must be aligned to 2 * PixPerClk * 4 bytes.
MemFmt :
Memory color format (XVidC_ColorFormat) to be used for reading frames.
StrmOut :
Pointer to the output video stream structure (XVidC_VideoStream).
This function validates the provided memory format, stride, and stream parameters, ensuring they are compatible with the hardware and the selected memory format. It checks for alignment, format support, and matching between memory and streaming formats. If all checks pass, it configures the hardware registers for the frame buffer reader with the specified parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the configuration is successful.
XVFRMBUFRD_ERR_FRAME_SIZE_INVALID if the frame size is invalid for the selected format.
XVFRMBUFRD_ERR_STRIDE_MISALIGNED if the stride is not properly aligned.
XVFRMBUFRD_ERR_VIDEO_FORMAT_MISMATCH if the memory and stream formats do not match.
XVFRMBUFRD_ERR_DISABLED_IN_HW if the selected memory format is not supported in hardware.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a pointer to the video stream associated with the given Frame Buffer Read instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the XVidC_VideoStream structure associated with the instance.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_SetBufferAddr - Set the frame buffer address for the Frame Buffer Read core.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr :
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
Addr :
Physical address of the frame buffer to be set.
This function sets the frame buffer address for the Frame Buffer Read core. It checks if the provided address is aligned to the required AXI memory width, which is calculated as 2 * PixelsPerClock * 4 bytes. If the address is not properly aligned, the function returns an error code indicating misalignment. If the address is valid and aligned, it writes the address to the hardware register.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the address is set successfully. XVFRMBUFRD_ERR_MEM_ADDR_MISALIGNED if the address is misaligned. XST_FAILURE for other failures.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon

simplesect.para: Address of buffer in memory

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address for the V plane for 3 planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the absolute address of buffer in memory

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE

briefdescription.para: This function reads the buffer address for the V plane for 3 planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon

simplesect.para: Address of buffer in memory

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address for the UV plane for semi-planar formats or Only U Plane for 3 planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the absolute address of buffer in memory

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE

briefdescription.para: This function reads the buffer address for the UV plane for semi-planar formats or Only U plane for 3 planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon

simplesect.para: Address of buffer in memory

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_SetFieldID - Sets the Field ID for the Frame Buffer Read hardware.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
FieldID
Field ID value to set (typically 0 or 1 for interlaced operation).
This function writes the specified Field ID value to the hardware register controlling the field selection for interlaced video modes. It asserts that the instance pointer is valid and that the hardware is configured for interlaced mode.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Field ID is set successfully, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_GetFieldID - Retrieves the current Field ID from the Frame Buffer Read hardware.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The current Field ID value as read from the hardware register.
This function asserts that the InstancePtr is not NULL and that the Frame Buffer Read instance is configured for interlaced mode.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_Set_FidOutMode - Sets the Field ID Output Mode for the Frame Buffer Read core.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
fidmodeval
Field ID output mode value (must be 0, 1, or 2).
This function configures the Field ID Output Mode register of the Frame Buffer Read hardware. It asserts that the instance pointer is valid, the fidmodeval is within the allowed range, and that the core is configured for interlaced operation.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation is successful, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the Field ID Output Mode (FidOutMode) from the Frame Buffer Read core instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads the current FidOutMode hardware register value from the specified XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance. It asserts that the instance pointer is not NULL and that the instance is configured for interlaced mode before accessing the register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The value of the FidOutMode hardware register.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_Get_FidErrorOut - Retrieves the Field ID (FID) error status from the Frame Buffer Read core.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The value of the FID error output register. Non-zero indicates an FID error.
This function asserts that the InstancePtr is not NULL and that the Frame Buffer Read core is configured for interlaced mode.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_Get_FidErrorCount - Retrieves the Field ID (FID) error count from the Frame Buffer Read core.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The number of FID errors detected by the hardware.
This function asserts that the InstancePtr is not NULL and that the Frame Buffer Read core is configured for interlaced mode.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current field output value from the Frame Buffer Read hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads the hardware register associated with the field output and returns its value, masked with the appropriate control bits. It asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL and that the instance is configured for interlaced mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The value of the field output as a 32-bit unsigned integer.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufRd_DbgReportStatus - Prints the current status and configuration of the Frame Buffer Read core.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufRd_l2 instance.
This function outputs a detailed debug report of the Frame Buffer Read (FrmbufRd) core's configuration and runtime status to the console using xil_printf. The report includes information such as pixel format support, color depth, AXI-MM data width, enabled formats, control register value, frame dimensions, stride, video format, and buffer addresses.
This function is intended for debugging and diagnostic purposes.

Preconditions:
InstancePtr must not be NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- (XVFRMBUFRD_HANDLER_DONE) DoneCallback (XVFRMBUFRD_HANDLER_READY) ReadyCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Frame Buffer Read core instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.
specifies the type of handler.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the frame buffer read core driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler clears the pending interrupt and determined if the source is frame done signal. If yes, starts the next frame processing and calls the registered callback function

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XVFrmbufRd_SetCallback() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance that just interrupted.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Frame Buffer Read Layer-2 Driver.

detaileddescription.para: The functions in this file provides interrupt handler and associated functions that are consumed by layer-2.

The functions in this file provides an abstraction from the register peek/poke methodology by implementing most common use-case provided by the sub-core. See xv_frmbufrd_l2.h for a detailed description of the layer-2 driver

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 vyc 04/05/17 Initial Release 2.00 vyc 10/04/17 Add second buffer pointer for semi-planar formats Add memory formats RGBA8, YUVA8, BGRA8, BGRX8, UYVY8 3.00 vyc 04/04/18 Add interlaced support Add new memory format BGR8 Add interrupt handler for ap_ready 4.00 pv 11/10/18 Added flushing feature support in driver. flush bit should be set and held (until reset) by software to flush pending transactions.IP is expecting a hard reset, when flushing is done.(There is a flush status bit and is asserted when the flush is done). 4.10 vv 02/05/19 Added new pixel formats with 12 and 16 bpc. 4.50 pg 01/07/21 Added new registers to support fid_out interlace solution. Interrupt count support for throughput measurement. 4.70 pg 05/23/23 Added new 3 planar video format Y_U_V8_420.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 vyc 04/05/17 Initial Release 3.00 vyc 04/04/18 Add interrupt handler for ap_ready 4.20 pg 01/31/20 Removed Frmbuf start function from Interrupt handler. 4.50 pg 01/07/21 Added new registers to support fid_out interlace solution. Interrupt count support for throughput measurement.
